front: Novel: foundling raised by Squire Allworthy; turns out to be Allworthy's nephew via Bridget
Novel: heroine's father (drunken boor obsessed with hunting) locks her in room; she runs away leaving muff on hero's bed
Novel: sleeps with Mrs. Waters at Upton inn; fears incest when she is revealed to be Jenny Jones
Novel: rival Master Blifil is the hero's half-brother; Mr. Square recants Blifil's lies near the end
Novel: contrasting tutors — philosophical Square and dogmatic student-beater Thwackum
Novel: Mr. Partridge (barber-surgeon, suspected father) criticizes David Garrick's acting as too realistic
Novel: mini-essays open each of 18 books; narrator bids farewell comparing readers to fellow coach passengers
Novel: chapter mimics Homer's style for graveyard battle where Molly fends off churchgoers with a thighbone
Novel: Lady Bellaston and Lord Fellamar plot against Sophia; Fellamar nearly assaults her
Novel: parody of Richardson's Pamela; Mrs. Booby exclaims 'Your virtue? I'll never survive it!'
Novel: Parson Abraham Adams (Aeschylus fan), chased by hunting dogs in Homeric combat parody; mentors Pamela's 'brother'
Novel: title character recognized as Mr. Wilson's son by a strawberry birthmark on his chest
Novel: title character resists Lady Booby; marries Fanny Goodwill; subtitled 'in imitation of Cervantes'
Author: founded the Bow Street Runners; later replaced by Metropolitan Police under Robert Peel
Author: Licensing Act of 1737 (passed by Walpole) drove this author from theater to novels
Author: Tom Thumb caricatures Walpole; tiny hero wins Princess Huncamunca, swallowed by red cow
Author: sister Sarah wrote The Governess (first English children's novel) and David Simple
Novel: William Booth imprisoned in Newgate; Amelia's nose ruined in carriage accident; parallels with the Aeneid
Author: wrote The Welsh Opera and Grub Street Opera under pseudonym Scriblerus Secundus
Author: wrote An Enquiry Into the Causes of the Late Increase of Robbers; campaigned against gin
Novel: hero impregnates Black George's daughter Molly Seagrim; Mr. Square later found sleeping with her
